answer:
\boxed{\text{polio vaccine}}

Hilary Koprowski was a key figure in early polio vaccine research, and his 1950 trials at Letchworth Village (a facility for people with disabilities) were part of the development of the **oral polio vaccine (OPV)**, later refined by Albert Sabin. While Jonas Salk’s inactivated vaccine was first licensed in 1955, Koprowski’s work contributed to the broader effort to combat polio. The question likely refers to this vaccine due to its historical context.
